content:
SECTION 1: Claim Submission

1. When MRC is Required:
   - Treatment in emergency at a non-empanelled hospital.
   - Expenditure incurred for treatment at an empanelled hospital.

2. Where to Submit:
   - Serving employees: Submit to concerned department.
   - Pensioners: Submit to CMO In-Charge of CGHS Wellness Centre:
       • Where CGHS card is registered, OR
       • Where treatment was taken.

3. Time Limit:
   - Claim must be submitted within 6 months from date of discharge.

4. Delay Beyond 6 Months:
   - Reasons must be clearly stated in a forwarding letter.
   - Request for condonation of delay must be included.

------------------------------------------------------------

SECTION 2: Acknowledgement and Claim Tracking

1. Claim Submission Location:
   - CGHS Wellness Centre where beneficiary is registered.

2. Verification:
   - Checked as per official checklist.
   - If complete, acknowledgement generated with claim number.

3. Tracking:
   - Claim status viewable using claim number in CGHS computer module.
   - SMS notifications sent at each stage of processing.

------------------------------------------------------------

SECTION 3: General Checklist for MRC (For Every Claim)

All documents must be self-attested and enclosed in the sequence below.
All pages must be numbered.

1. Computer generated MRC Number.
2. Self-explanatory forwarding letter:
   - Signed by main card holder.
   - Forwarded by CMO I/C.
   - Detailed justification.
   - Reason for choosing non-empanelled hospital (if applicable).
   - Delay explanation (if beyond 6 months).
   - If unable to sign:
       • Right thumb impression (female).
       • Left thumb impression (male).
       • Medical certificate if medically unfit to sign.

3. Photocopy of CGHS card (claimant and patient), verified by CMO I/C.
   - Mention email ID and mobile number.

4. Mandate Form (Mandatory), along with:
   a. Cancelled cheque with claimant name, OR
   b. Copy of passbook showing account number and claimant name, OR
   c. Bank-verified mandate form if name not printed on cheque.

5. Original permission letter OR original emergency certificate.
6. Discharge summary (original or copy).
7. Copy of referral/advice from specialist (if applicable).
8. Final consolidated hospital bill (original).
9. Detailed break-up bill (interim bill not valid).
10. Original receipts of total payment made.

    Additional conditions:
    - Implants/devices invoice must include batch number and specifications.
    - If duplicate receipt submitted: affidavit for lost receipt required.

11. List of all bills/receipts with numbers and total claimed amount.
12. Declaration whether advance taken (Yes/No).
    - If yes: utilization certificate from hospital required.

------------------------------------------------------------

SECTION 4: Beneficiary Instructions

- Number all pages in prescribed sequence.
- Make photocopy of entire claim.
- Retain one set for record.
- Submit original MRC to Wellness Centre.
- If resubmitting after objection, fresh documents must be submitted in duplicate.
- Do not delete CGHS card until final payment is received (in death-related cases).

------------------------------------------------------------

SECTION 5: Special Case Checklist

5.1 Lost Original Bill
   - Affidavit on non-judicial stamp paper (as per Annexure I).
   - Photocopies verified by treating specialist.

5.2 Partial Credit Cases
   - Complete final bill with break-up.
   - Break-up bill for items not credited.

------------------------------------------------------------

SECTION 6: Death Cases

Eligibility:

1. Death of Main Card Holder (Pensioner):
   - Only living spouse eligible claimant.
   - Recommended: Obtain family pensioner card before claim submission.

2. Death of Family Pensioner (Spouse):
   - Any living child may claim.
   - Requires affidavit as legal heir.
   - Separate NOC from other heirs (Annexure II).

3. No Living Spouse or Children:
   - Succession certificate from court required.
   - Proof of payment to hospital required.

Documents Required:
   - Affidavit (Annexure II).
   - NOC from all legal heirs.
   - Death certificate.
   - Death summary from hospital.
   - ID proof of claimant.
   - Succession certificate (if applicable).

------------------------------------------------------------

SECTION 7: Device / Implant Specific Requirements

7.1 Cataract Surgery with IOL
   - Original IOL sticker with batch number.
   - IOL bill with batch number.
   - Discharge summary must mention:
       • Type of IOL
       • Type of cataract surgery performed

7.2 Cardiac / Vascular Stents
   - Outer pouch with batch sticker.
   - Invoice with batch number.
   - Certificate confirming rate compliance.
   - Angiography report.
   - CD of angiography/PTCA.

7.3 Pacemaker / Defibrillator / Combo Device / Rotablator
   - Device sticker with batch number.
   - Warranty terms.
   - ECG, Holter report.
   - ECHO report.
   - Warranty copy of earlier device (if replacement).

7.4 Knee & Hip Implants
   - Component-wise cost.
   - Brand name.
   - Manufacturer/importer.
   - Batch number and specifications.
   - Ceiling rates applicable.

------------------------------------------------------------

SECTION 8: Other Special Categories

8.1 Ambulance (Emergency Only)
   - Allowed only for hospital admission in emergency.
   - Not allowed after discharge.
   - Doctor certificate confirming:
       • Emergency condition
       • Other transport would endanger life
       • Within city limits

8.2 Special Nurse / Aya / Attendant
   - Permitted only for:
       • Government hospitals, OR
       • Recognized private hospitals with prior permission
   - No reimbursement for domiciliary attendant.
   - Doctor certificate required.

8.3 Domiciliary Physiotherapy
   Requires:
   - Specialist prescription including:
       • Type of therapy
       • Technical personnel
       • Frequency
       • Duration
   - Original payment receipt.
   - Disability certificate (>80%) OR certification by two Govt specialists.

8.4 Medicines for 7 Days on Discharge
   - Only if hospital did not provide medicines.
   - Certificate from hospital required.
   - OPD medicines not reimbursable unless written permission by CMO I/C.

8.5 Insurance Cases
   - Claim must first be submitted to insurance company.
   - Insurance certificate showing credited amount.
   - Certified copies of bills.

8.6 Nebulizer
   - Government specialist advice.
   - Undertaking: not procured at Govt expense in last 5 years.
   - Purchase receipt.

8.7 Hearing Aid
   - Original permission letter.
   - Empty carton with seller details.
   - Copy of CGHS card.
   - Original bill.
   - Box with product details.

------------------------------------------------------------

KEY TERMS:
- MRC: Medical Reimbursement Claim
- CMO I/C: Chief Medical Officer In-Charge
- CGHS: Central Government Health Scheme
